menu.title: DARKLINE — First Island Chain
brief.title: Briefing
brief.body:
TAIPEI — 1953. AFTER DARK

Third floor, West-Marches Trading Co. On paper, we clear cargo for the Americans — manifests, customs, freight.

In truth this loft is the knot in a net: dead drops, cipher pads, the rules of contact, tying together the men this city never sees.

Every codename in the book is a life. One rule only: no name on paper, no face in the open.
brief.body2:
Old Nieh pushes a stack of return-slips across the table.

"The outer lamps are going dark. One by one." "Three weeks. Five codenames. Silence."

He doesn't look up. "The North may be inside the city now. Find who's hunting our people. Close the leak."

At the door he stops me, his eyes on the name on my papers, and says, softly: "Chien-kuo. 'Found a nation.' A fine name. Everyone hears it the same way — a loyal son of the Republic."

I said nothing. Some words you can only read, in private, as another country.
decode.title: Decode Cable
decode.needkey: No cipher key — find what the contact left in the alley first.
decode.clue: The cable opens up: the handoff is at Pier 3, the midnight watch — and the North has put a price on every name. The last line isn't about tonight: "If the line is cut, reconnect along the old net, await no order." The old net… some lines are buried precisely to wake decades later.
scrap.title: Dead Drop
scrap.body:
Behind the loose brick in the corner, a scrap in Old Zhou's hand, oil still on the edge:
"Same key as ever — cipher {c} is plain {p}. Set that one, the rest opens itself."
card.dropoff.title: The Drop-off
card.dropoff.body:
The headlights die at the alley mouth. This is Dadaocheng — herb-shop sacks still stacked under the arcade, tea and rain in the air.

The outer men are going silent one by one, but the deaths are wrong: not robbed of intel, but their property seized "by law." The North doesn't work this way.

Go in. Tonight's answer is hidden among these back doors and dead drops.
card.embark.title: To the Docks
card.embark.body:
The scrap from the dead drop lines up. The thread doesn't point north — it points back inside West-Marches, back to Hsiao Ching-chih. It's our own, "settling" our own.

But the Northern deal is real, and it's at the docks tonight; the horn is already drawing out.

The fight still has to be fought — and even won, it only settles half. Follow the band of light at the alley's end.
free.exit: Head for the docks (reach the end of the alley)
ending.title: Mission Complete
ending.body:
The list was saved. The men were not.

The "confession" Old Nieh signed became the foundation of the whole case — they used a good man's hand to bury a line of good men.

On the edge of the city there is a house. The general, sidelined into an honorary post, now lives under guard. They say his wife grows roses in the yard, and sells them at the market.

A man decorated by three nations lost, in the end, not to the enemy — but to his own, afraid of a man who could win without them.
ending.body2:
I sealed the true list — and the proof the confession was beaten out of him — into a dead drop only the future can open.

The general left me one line. I ciphered it in: "Names endure. The men, they can take. The names — you must carry out."

Beside the struck-through codename I added one more line, half for those who come after, half — for the country I never said aloud:

          UNTIL THE FIRST ISLAND CHAIN TIGHTENS AGAIN

From tonight, my name is not in the book. They will call me — Darkline.

—— 1996 · THE STRAIT · to be continued ——
over.title: Mission Failed
over.body: The line is cut. The roster is in Northern hands.
over.retry: (Press R to retry from the last checkpoint)
holding.title: DARKLINE
holding.body: Taipei, 1953 — best experienced in a desktop browser.
